entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics, the organization managing plant germplasm exchange

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the described plant quarantine procedures are implemented by ICRISAT

entities.name: PLANT GERMPLASM EXCHANGES
entities.description: The movement of plant genetic materials between countries for research and improvement

entities.name: CROP IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: The objective of exchanging plant germplasm

entities.name: PLANT QUARANTINE INSPECTIONS
entities.description: The set of procedures and safeguards used to ensure the safe import and export of plant materials

entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The crop (Arachis sp.) used as an example for quarantine procedures

entities.name: INTERMEDIATE QUARANTINE
entities.description: A specific safeguard method used for imported groundnut cuttings

entities.name: FUMIGATION
entities.description: A chemical treatment method used to eliminate pests and pathogens in seeds

entities.name: DRY SEED INSPECTION
entities.description: A method of examining seeds in their dry state for contaminants

entities.name: MICROSCOPIC EXAMINATION
entities.description: A method of using microscopes to detect pathogens in plant materials

entities.name: SEED TREATMENT
entities.description: The application of treatments to seeds to ensure they are disease-free

entities.name: QUARANTINE ISOLATION AREA
entities.description: A controlled location where crops are grown to verify they are disease-free

entities.name: ELISA
entities.description: Enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay, a biochemical method used to detect groundnut viruses

entities.name: GROUNDNUT VIRUSES
entities.description: Pathogenic organisms that affect groundnut crops and are screened during export

entities.name: PACKING
entities.description: The process of securing seeds for safe and healthy export

entities.name: DISEASE FREE
entities.description: The state of being free from pathogenic infections
